Value: Large Salad Plate/Bowl, Bev Napkin Liner
Wash oysters in cold water, scrubbing the outside shell and hinge thoroughly
In a pot of boiling water, blanch seaweed for ~30 seconds or until bright and green Shock.
Pre-shuck the oysters and reserve on ice, in a cooler, for no longer than an hour before use
Shave ice and reserve in ice well for use in pick up
When preparing the Cedar Paper for the filet, reserve the 1x4 inch strip.
Burn the Catch logo into the Cedar Paper strip and reserve in a cool, dry place.
In the plate, place bev napkin first then cover with shaved ice to just over the top of the plate lip
Place seaweed naturally on top of the ice, not touching the edges of the plate.
Line the oysters around the outside edge of the ice, just to the lip of the edge of the plate.
Poke the bamboo leaf into the ice near the middle of the plate in a decorative fold, then place lemons next
Poke the catch logo paper into the ice between the bamboo and oysters, with the logo visible
Garnish with ramekins of cocktail sauce and mignonette (Runners) and serve immediately.
